jokes.text: There's a person here. You mean you, Jim? Uh... Yes, I mean me. I have something to say and your ignoring me made me forget it!
jokes.setup: Jim interrupts a conversation to assert his presence
jokes.punchline: He immediately admits he forgot what he wanted to say
jokes.explanation: Classic character-driven comedy - Jim's need for attention immediately undercut by his own incompetence. Well-structured with good timing.

jokes.text: Guess I told him, didn't I? You sure did. I just hope he had it coming.
jokes.setup: Jim thinks he successfully confronted someone
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry response suggests Jim may have been inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Nice understated follow-up that suggests Jim's victory might be hollow. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: That puts you on our 'A' list.
jokes.setup: New driver Nina shows her license
jokes.punchline: Louie acts like having a basic legal requirement is impressive
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie sarcasm about low standards. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I got my good side and I got my bad side. Yeah, his good side is he's never bombed the children's hospital.
jokes.setup: Louie claims he has a good side
jokes.punchline: Alex suggests his 'good side' is just not committing heinous acts
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurdity. Perfect setup/punchline structure with dark humor that defines both characters.

jokes.text: You gonna listen to me or you gonna stand there looking contemporary?
jokes.setup: Nina is distracted or fashionably dressed
jokes.punchline: 'Contemporary' as an insult instead of 'pretty' or similar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant word choice that's both insulting and confusing. Very quotable and uniquely Louie's voice.

jokes.text: There are no men and women in this garage. Only hacks who ferry frightened citizens around a diseased city.
jokes.setup: Nina laughs at a joke between the drivers
jokes.punchline: Louie's cynical worldview strips away all humanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Louie's nihilistic management style. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: His mother dropped him on his head. About a week ago.
jokes.setup: Standard insult about childhood head injury
jokes.punchline: Twist that it happened recently, not as a baby
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with perfect timing. Takes familiar insult and adds unexpected twist.

jokes.text: I see you're planning on driving in the borough of Oz. Watch out for the Munchkins; they're light tippers.
jokes.setup: Nina has unrealistic expectations about happy taxi customers
jokes.punchline: Reference to Wizard of Oz with practical taxi driver concern
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that grounds Nina's optimism in reality. Well-constructed but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Let's see, uh, you got cab 404. That's got no springs and no heater. We call it Old Whip 'n' Chill.
jokes.setup: Nina deserves something 'special' for her first night
jokes.punchline: Louie gives her the worst cab with a punny name
jokes.explanation: Good pun that captures both the cab's problems and Louie's sadistic nature. Memorable name.

jokes.text: You're cute, Jim. Ah... What a kidder! What a kidder. Maybe you're right.
jokes.setup: Jim asks who Elaine thinks is cute
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sarcastic response and Jim's earnest acceptance of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Jim's obliviousness to sarcasm is perfectly in character. His final line sells the bit.

jokes.text: You know, one show almost got to Broadway called Stompin' and Clompin' and Cuttin' Loose. It played one week and I lost 11 pounds.
jokes.setup: Nina describes her theatrical experience
jokes.punchline: The show's failure measured by her dramatic weight loss
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Nina's struggles while maintaining her upbeat attitude.

jokes.text: Yeah, every script they send you has you saying either 'jive turkey' or 'say what, sucker?' How'd you know that?
jokes.setup: Alex predicts the type of TV roles Nina gets offered
jokes.punchline: Nina's amazement at his accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good observation about stereotypical roles for black actresses in the era. Shows Alex's perceptiveness.

jokes.text: I have this little alarm that goes off every time I'm with someone who's about to be enthusiastic.
jokes.setup: Alex is tired and doesn't want to talk
jokes.punchline: His cynical way of describing Nina's energy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex line - world-weary but clever. Shows his defensive mechanism against optimism.

jokes.text: I mean, I-I-I'm really too tired to talk, let alone participate in a conversation with a young actress cab driver who's about to lick the world.
jokes.setup: Alex is tired
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific description of what he can't handle
jokes.explanation: 'Lick the world' is a great phrase that captures Nina's attitude. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: Sorry's no good when there's no Eiffel Tower.
jokes.setup: Passenger complains about being late to decorating committee
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific complaint about missing decoration
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random line that suggests elaborate party planning gone wrong. Memorable for its specificity.

jokes.text: We're 16 and we're in love, so could you please do us a favor? Yeah. What? Buy us liquor? I would, but I forgot my I.D.
jokes.setup: Sweet young couple asks for a favor
jokes.punchline: They want alcohol, Alex deflects with absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and Alex's response is perfectly awkward. Well-structured bit.

jokes.text: Nee-na/Nine-a's Magical Mystery Tour.
jokes.setup: Nina has picked up multiple passengers and caused chaos
jokes.punchline: Alex compares it to Beatles' psychedelic adventure
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pop culture reference that captures the chaotic ride experience.

jokes.text: If I had a car, I would have asked Wendy.
jokes.setup: Passenger complains about not having a car
jokes.punchline: Reveals he would have preferred someone else anyway
jokes.explanation: Minor throwaway line that adds to the teenagers' dynamic but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: I've never said this before, miss, but wacka, wacka, wacka!
jokes.setup: Nina brings in an envelope overflowing with money
jokes.punchline: Louie's bizarre exclamation of joy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Louie moment. The nonsense exclamation is both funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: We got matching shoes in there.
jokes.setup: Louie tells Nina she can take anything from lost and found
jokes.punchline: The specific mention of matching shoes suggests mostly junk
jokes.explanation: Good detail that suggests the lost and found is full of random, useless items.

jokes.text: With her skinny body and your nose, they'd spend their whole lives falling on their faces.
jokes.setup: Louie talks about Alex and Nina having kids
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of their combined physical traits
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insult with vivid imagery. Mean but funny and well-constructed.

jokes.text: She left these tied to the cab door handle with a note saying, 'It's about time you started cheering up. I'll be helpful.'
jokes.setup: Alex complains about Nina brightening his life
jokes.punchline: Balloons left on his cab with overly helpful note
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows Nina's persistent optimism and Alex's discomfort with it.

jokes.text: My heart belongs to Cabbie.
jokes.setup: Nina wrote a message on Alex's dirty windshield
jokes.punchline: She wrote a love message but used his job title instead of his name
jokes.explanation: Cute detail showing Nina's romantic but slightly impersonal gesture. The dirt writing is a nice touch.

jokes.text: There's not enough helium in this world.
jokes.setup: Elaine wishes she had Nina's confidence to be direct
jokes.punchline: Suggests Nina's buoyancy requires actual helium
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor connecting Nina's lightness to the balloons. Good wordplay and economy.

jokes.text: Oh, that's all right... Sometime they'll come back down and brighten up an otherwise dull day around here.
jokes.setup: Balloons fly away when Alex opens the door
jokes.punchline: Elaine's dry comment about their eventual return
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan follow-up to the balloon gag. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Look, the last thing I need in my life is a born-again cheerleader who's gonna try to kick some life into the old boy.
jokes.setup: Alex explains why he doesn't want to date Nina
jokes.punchline: 'Born-again cheerleader' as description of Nina's personality
jokes.explanation: Great phrase that perfectly captures Nina's relentless positivity and Alex's resistance to it.

jokes.text: She's like every actress or Hindu that I ever met. She has karma, she's high on life, she's positive, hard-working, joyful... and I don't like her!
jokes.setup: Alex lists Nina's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Despite acknowledging all her good traits, he still doesn't like her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Alex's self-awareness about his unreasonable position. Well-structured contradiction.

jokes.text: I'm not a sentimental man, but each day when I look into this envelope, I get all misty.
jokes.setup: Louie examines Nina's money envelope
jokes.punchline: Claims to be moved to tears by the cash
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie greed disguised as sentiment. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm not a sentimental man, but each day when I look into this envelope, I get all misty.
jokes.setup: Louie looking at Nina's daily earnings
jokes.punchline: Claims to get emotional over money, which is perfectly in character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie - the only thing that makes him sentimental is money

jokes.text: If she doesn't understand, you can explain it over a weekend in Connecticut.
jokes.setup: Alex says he's going to be direct with Nina about not dating
jokes.punchline: Elaine implies they'll end up together anyway
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy prediction. Functional setup for later developments.

jokes.text: What does it take to get you excited? I would get excited if you got the part. I think I've gotten the part. Oh, don't be ridiculous!
jokes.setup: Nina asks what would excite Alex
jokes.punchline: When she gives the answer he wanted, he immediately dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's pessimism even when he gets what he asked for.

jokes.text: I've never been so moved since Judy Garland re-opened at the Palace.
jokes.setup: Nina recounts what the director supposedly said
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top theatrical comparison that may be embellished
jokes.explanation: Shows Nina's tendency to possibly exaggerate or misremember compliments in the best possible light.

jokes.text: Stand back, Alex. I'm gonna need a little freakin' space!
jokes.setup: Nina gets the phone call about the part
jokes.punchline: She dramatically prepares for what she assumes will be good news
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the coming disappointment. Shows Nina's theatrical nature and confidence.

jokes.text: They said I was too right for it. Too... Too on the nose.
jokes.setup: Nina gets rejection call after being confident
jokes.punchline: Absurd theatrical rejection reason
jokes.explanation: Good example of show business doublespeak. The rejection reason is both specific and meaningless.

jokes.text: It's just me they didn't like.
jokes.setup: Nina explains they loved her acting, dancing, and singing
jokes.punchline: Devastating self-realization about personal rejection
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking line that's both funny and genuinely sad. Perfect encapsulation of show business cruelty.

jokes.text: The last broken heart I had was when the Jets didn't cover.
jokes.setup: Alex tells Nina she's lucky to have heartbreak over something meaningful
jokes.punchline: His last heartbreak was over sports betting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex line - self-deprecating and reveals his cynical worldview while making his point about Nina's passion.

jokes.text: I'm a no-magic man.
jokes.setup: Alex explains the basic problem between him and Nina
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect distillation of Alex's character in four words. Honest, sad, and funny simultaneously.

jokes.text: You said no magic, and darned if it ain't so!
jokes.setup: Nina and Alex kiss goodnight
jokes.punchline: Nina confirms Alex's assessment after an unromantic kiss
jokes.explanation: Great callback that turns Alex's self-deprecation into a punchline. Shows Nina can be witty too.

jokes.text: You said no magic, and darned if it ain't so!
jokes.setup: Alex kisses Nina goodnight after saying he's no-magic
jokes.punchline: Nina confirms the kiss was indeed unmagical
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides closure to both the romantic tension and Alex's self-description. Bittersweet humor.

jokes.text: You've got a lot more problems than that. / I'm fine. / No, I mean it. I'm fine. / I'm fine.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to offer help, Nina insists she's fine
jokes.punchline: The repetition reveals she's clearly not fine, and Alex's persistence makes it more awkward
jokes.explanation: Good awkward ending showing both characters struggling with the situation. The repetition works for the discomfort.
